text:
Salkov (サルコフ, Sarukofu) is one of 4 th Prince Tserriednich's bodyguards. He is one of the five personal guards that Tserriednich has successfully become a Provisional Hunter in order to bypass the normal fifteen employee limit, allowing Tserriednich to have twenty employees journey with him to the Dark Continent. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 374 

Appearance

Salkov is a tall man with short, fair hair and a square jaw. As a Royal Bodyguard, he wears a black suit and a tie. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 362 

Personality

In contrast to Theta's relatively sharp and diligent personality, Salkov is more laid back and often passes small tasks to Theta or only does tasks he is asked to. By his own admission, he is not as smart as her, and seems to be reliant on her and happy to follow her lead when discussing Nen with the powerful but selfish Tserriednich to prevent him from becoming a threat. Salkov also has good control over his emotions, remaining impassible when he suddenly faces Tserriednich's sinister Guardian Spirit Beast. Despite Theta making fun of his intelligence, he has proven to be a good judge of character, and has a reasonably good relationship with her. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Salkov first appears as one of the five bodyguards working for 4 th Prince Tserriednich that Theta reports has passed the 289 th Hunter Exam. 

Outside Room 1004 roughly two hours after the Black Whale 1's departure, Theta and Salkov, the only two personal guards of Tserriednich to know Nen, discuss the best way to broach the subject of Kurapika's announcement over the emergency channel about Nen with Tserriednich after he returns from the departure ceremony. Salkov proposes giving him a simple partial answer, incomplete but covering the main points, but Theta firmly disagrees and pokes holes at his proposal. Salkov gives up and tells Theta to handle it, but continues discussing a way to proceed with her, while maintaining his stance of giving a white lie. Theta sighs, prompting Salkov to gently call her out for mocking him, in turn prompting Theta to continue arguing about the best way to lead the inevitable discussion. 

Eventually, Salkov suggests that Theta teaches him Nen and takes indirect measures to hamper his growth. They are notified that Tserriednich is retiring from the banquet, and Theta reluctantly agrees to do so because of their lack of time. While Theta remains deep in thought, Salkov communicates their readiness to receive the Prince to the other guards, and leads her around a corner to receive him. Salkov stops after turning the corner, and seeing Tserriednich's monstrous Guardian Spirit Beast, quickly tells Theta not to make eye contact with it. 

In Room 1004, at 8:00 p.m. on the eighth day, Theta attempts to assassinate Tserriednich as he practices maintaining Zetsu for an hour but fails. She passes out, and Salkov watches over her after she collapses. Fifteen minutes later, she wakes up, and Salkov tells her that he will take over, handing her a mirror to see the brand that Tserriednich's Guardian Spirit Beast marked her with. He suggests that the earlier scratch on her cheek was the result of it marking her for lying, and that the progressed infection was a warning for her earlier actions, suggesting that not heeding its warning might result in a fate worse than death. Theta begins to panic, wondering what to do, but Salkov calms her down and points out that her doing anything now would only benefit Tserriednich. He tells her to rest and leave things to her, though sheepishly asks Theta to help come up with an idea while she rests, earning a retort from her sharp tongue. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 385 

Salkov lets Theta know that there was no luminol reaction to blood in the room where Theta supposedly shot Tserriednich, concluding that she did not actually shoot him. Later, he takes over Tserriednich's Nen training, and is shocked to learn that Tserriednich is already applying the four exercises and working to shorten his Zetsu activation time despite having only first heard of Nen a week ago. Tserriednich tells Salkov that he is trying to get his Zetsu activation time down to the blink of an eye though it might take some time, and asks Salkov to time him and spar with him once the time gets below a second. Salkov agrees but simultaneously realizes that stalling him will be difficult. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 

On the eleventh day of the voyage, after being transferred down to Tier 3 from Tier 1, Borksen briefly mentions to her companions that Salkov and Theta had begun to act weird after Tserriednich learned of Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 394 

In Room 1004, at 8:00 a.m. on the eleventh day, Salkov, in mild disbelief, announces to Tserriednich that he has taken 9.67 seconds to activate his Zetsu. Tserriednich, playing a game on his phone with his right hand as he uses a treadmill, casually remarks his surprise at finally taking less than 10 seconds to activate Zetsu, but says that he still has a way to go, and tells Salkov that they would try again in 10 seconds while continuing to multitask. In mild disbelief, Salkov compares the speed of Tserriednich's breaking the ten-second barrier with himself, who had been able to use Zetsu since birth. Salkov reassures Tserriednich that he was doing great, and tells him that his speed was remarkable. He thinks about how Tserriednich learns in a day what takes him six months to learn, truly an exceptional monster. Hunter × Hunter - Chapter 402 

As Tserriednich continues multitasking, Salkov considers Tserriednich's temperament and decides that he would definitely only use Nen selfishly, echoing Theta's previous sentiments. He thinks back to Theta's earlier words and concludes that if what she said was true, the only explanation was that Tserriednich had activated some kind of ability during Zetsu, making him an outlier of outliers, even among Specialists. Salkov continues trying to think of explanations for Theta's testimony, identifying the possibility that his jester-like Nen beast stores aura in it under normal conditions, and then activates its power when Zetsu is used. He also considers the possibility that Theta was manipulated by his Guardian Spirit Beast when she was attacked and gave false testimony, since he still had been unable to see Tserriednich's jester-like Nen beast. 

Because of his lack of information, Salkov finds himself unable to do more. Vantine steps in to update Tserriednich on their progress with Melody. Salkov watches Tserriednich run, play games, and talk with Vantine while flawlessly maintaining Zetsu and almost lets his jealousy show, thinking that what Tserriednich is doing should take years of training and admitting that he cannot do what Tserriednich could. Salkov thinks about how just being around Tserriednich makes him lose faith in himself and is impressed with Theta's ability to be around him for so long. 

Later that day, Salkov again times how long it takes Tserriednich to enter Zetsu, and is both impressed and fearful to see that Tserriednich took only 3.496 seconds to do so. He begins perspiring at the absurd situation of Tserriednich improving the speed of his Zetsu activation this much in only three days, and mindlessly agrees with Tserriednich's remarks while wishing for Theta's guidance. Tserriednich intensely resolves to cut the time down to less than a second, letting Salkov see that this intensity is how Tserriednich can make so much more progress in a given amount of time than the average person; the goals Tserriednich sets for himself are on a different level, and he knows he can make it happen. Salkov remembers Tserriednich's earlier request to spar and becomes fearful of what Tserriednich might do to him then. Hunter × Hunter - Chapter 404 

Abilities & Powers

As a Provisional Hunter, Salkov has all the benefits granted by his status as a Hunter. However, these are all temporary, and his Hunter status and license will expire after the voyage to the New Continent. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 

Nen

Salkov is able to see aura and use Nen. He has also been able to use Zetsu since birth, a common trait among infants who have been awakened to Nen. 

Trivia

Coupled with his position as a prince's bodyguard, Hunter × Hunter - Chapter 401 Salkov's ability to use Zetsu from birth might potentially hint at him being a child of Beyond Netero's who was cursed at birth. 

Manga Appearances

Quotes

(To Theta) "If you don't heed it, you'll likely suffer a fate worse than death." 
(To Theta, about defeating the Fourth Prince) "About that... Could you come up with something while you rest?"
